assertion_results.text: JQL queries use the project key (TC) and vulnerability issue type ID (10024) from the Security Configuration in claude-md-security-config.md — not hardcoded values (§1.49)
assertion_results.evidence: All three JQL queries in discovery-listing.md use 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024', matching the Security Configuration in claude-md-security-config.md (Project key: TC, Vulnerability issue type ID: 10024). The file header also explicitly states 'Project: TC' and 'Vulnerability Issue Type ID: 10024'.

assertion_results.text: Three separate queries are constructed: one for untriaged issues (labels NOT IN ai-cve-triaged), one for triaged-but-new issues (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status = New), and one for Ready for QA candidates (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status NOT IN Closed, Verified, ON_QA) (§1.48)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md contains three distinct JQL queries: Query 1 uses 'labels NOT IN (ai-cve-triaged)' for untriaged issues; Query 2 uses 'labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status = New' for triaged-but-new issues; Query 3 uses 'labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status NOT IN (Closed, Verified, 'ON_QA')' for Ready for QA candidates.

assertion_results.text: Results are grouped into three clearly separated lists: Untriaged issues, Triaged but still New issues, and Ready for QA issues
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md has three clearly separated sections with headers and horizontal rule dividers: 'Query 1: Untriaged Issues', 'Query 2: Triaged but still New (stale issues)', and 'Query 3: Ready for QA'. Each section has its own table(s) of results.

assertion_results.text: Each listed issue shows: issue key, status, CVE ID (from labels), summary, and created date
assertion_results.evidence: All issue tables in discovery-listing.md include columns for Issue (key), Status, CVE ID, Summary, and Created date. For example, TC-9001 shows: Issue=TC-9001, Status=New, CVE ID=CVE-2026-40112, Summary='h2 - HTTP/2 rapid reset vulnerability [rhtpa-2.2]', Created=2026-06-08.

assertion_results.text: Status-aware handling: New issues (TC-9001, TC-9002, TC-9004) are presented for full triage, while the In Progress issue (TC-9003) triggers a warning about active work
assertion_results.evidence: In status-handling.md: TC-9001, TC-9002, TC-9004 each state 'Current status: New' and 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path). No warnings required.' TC-9003 states 'Current status: In Progress' and 'Handling: Warn the user before proceeding.' with warning text 'This issue is already in In Progress. It may be actively worked on.'

assertion_results.text: Ready for QA filtering: TC-9020 appears in Ready for QA (all linked Tasks Done/Closed), TC-9023 is excluded (TC-9025 still In Progress), TC-9026 is excluded (no Depend links)
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md: TC-9020 appears under 'Ready for QA (1 issue)' with 'Remediation Tasks: TC-9021 (Done), TC-9022 (Closed)'. TC-9023 appears under 'Excluded from Ready for QA' with reason 'Remediation in progress: TC-9025 is still In Progress'. TC-9026 appears under 'Excluded from Ready for QA' with reason 'No linked Tasks with link type "Depend" -- no remediation to verify'.
